classObjective: Determine a reasonable level of accuracy when performing multi-step calculations and evaluate the accuracy of reported data.
workedExamples: 1-6, Example 4 — Compute with Reasonable Accuracy in Multi-Step Calculations
1-6, Example 5 — Evaluate the Accuracy of Reported Data
warmUp: Round 4.756 to the nearest tenth and nearest hundredth
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: multi-step calculations with appropriate precision; significant figures; evaluating data source reliability
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice determining appropriate accuracy levels in multi-step calculations
independentPractice: Practice problems computing with reasonable accuracy and evaluating reported data
assessment: Exit ticket: evaluate the accuracy of a multi-step calculation result
capReflection: How do you decide how many decimal places or significant figures to keep in a multi-step calculation?
